Jongpil Jeong

3D Optical Imaging Systems Lab.
Computer Science and Networks
Dept. of Creative Informatics
Graduate School of Computer Science and Systems Enginnering
Kyushu Institute of Technology

I am an incoming Researcher (starting April 2026) at the 3D Optical Imaging Systems Lab., Kyushu Institute of Technology, advised by Prof. Min-Chul Lee.

My research interests are in the field of image signal processing and computer vision.
I am currently working on solving visibility problem in extreme environments. My research related with:

Image Signal Processing (ISP)
  • Biomedical Imaging Systems
    • Digital Holographic Microscopy (DHM)
  • Three-dimentional Imaging Systems
    • Integral Imaging (II)
  • Image Enhancement
    • Deblurring
      • Peplography
      • Adaptive Removal via Mask for Scatter (ARMS)
    • Night Vision
      • Photon Counting Detection model
    • Noise Reduction
      • Kalman Filter
Computer Vision (CV)
  • Pre-hatching sex determination technique for chicken
    • Related Organization
      • Hitachi Solutions create, Ltd.
      • National Agriculture and Fodd Research Organization
      • Kyushu Institute of Technology
    • Related Article
      • AI Identifies Chicken Embryo Gender with 97% Accuracy to Cut Costs: Hitachi Affiliate and Partners Developed New Technology Link
      • Joint Development of Technology by HSC, NARO, and Kyushu Institute of Technology (Kyutech) for Nondestructive Pre-hatching Sex Determination for Eggs on the 3 Day of Incubation Link
      • Article pdf
Machine Learning / Deep Learning
  • Microdegree in Graduate School of Data Science at Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology (KAIST)
